title: Eigen 5.0 next door
author: stefano2734

title: I have a question regarding the usage of fit-model-multi.
author: julia6945
body:
I have a question regarding the usage of fit-model-multi.

Until now, I’ve only been using fit-model.
However, since I was using only a single image, the results sometimes appeared distorted, especially when using side-profile images.
I’ve attached an example image for reference.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/28e2e920-0b03-41f9-b4a0-66c54938bb24)

I’m wondering if using fit-model-multi could resolve this issue.
As a test, I tried the following command:
/*
fit-model-multi -m share/sfm_shape_3448.bin \
-i image1.jpg -l image1.pts \
-i image2.jpg -l image2.pts \
-p share/ibug_to_sfm.txt \
-c share/sfm_model_contours.json \
-e share/sfm_3448_edge_topology.json \
-b share/expression_blendshapes_3448.bin \
-o output_directory/
*/

However, it didn’t generate an .obj file.
Could someone please let me know if I’m doing something wrong?

Also, since East Asians tend to have different nose bridge heights,
I’m curious whether using multiple .jpg and .pts files with fit-model-multi would help in aligning the nose height more accurately.

Thank you very much!

title: Could aortic sinus be a mesh instead of the face mesh ? for TAVI surgical placement of valves
author: TaiPhillips
body:
background challenge

ct angiogram has flouroscopy causing contrast gains and losses, the aortic sinus is a location i want to localize as the video of the ct angiogram frames are processed - this needs to be object detection computer vision not using post processing ct from dicom files as that is not real time


my question

excuse me if this is a wrong question, but for real time video frames from ct angiogram which changes when fluroscopy is occuring and the contrast is naturally changing - i was wondering if instead of a face detection or mesh model, a aortic sinus 'mesh' may be the focus of a project i have - for TAVI surgery, so that as one side of the mesh (like one side of the face) has aortic points, the mesh applied to the real time (or post processing video) frames could help the 'mesh' as catheter wires and valves are sought to be localised by surgeons and if a 'mesh' of the aortis sinus (where the TAVI valve is being positioned) could exist in a display, it may help place and position the valve - by the mesh being visualized and 'updated' by processing the CT angiogram on the real time frames.

Hope someone can say 'hmm' if you do x, y, z, maybe... :)

Thank you kindly for reading - any advice or direction on the mesh'ing of an aortic sinus (as 1 side is all we see in ct angiograms in video feeds) is helpful.

title: Inquiry about the mouth shape in the Mesh
author: julia6945
body:
I have a question regarding the mouth shape in the generated mesh.
I used the fit-model tool with the following options to generate a .obj file:

/////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
fit-model -m share/sfm_shape_3448.bin -i Sample6.jpg -l Sample6_face_1_float.pts -p share/ibug_to_sfm.txt -c share/sfm_model_contours.json -e share/sfm_3448_edge_topology.json -b share/expression_blendshapes_3448.bin -o Sample6
/////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/70d9e5d0-4d37-4eff-b7bd-feee7bd0f01c)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/431a2f7c-0df8-45f0-83f4-99c7c55bd7d9)


However, the mouth in the result appears to be slightly open.
Is there any way to fix this issue?
I’ve attached the original photo and a captured image showing the slightly opened mouth.

If anyone knows how to solve this, I’d really appreciate your help.

title: Increase texturing area
author: AlKhwarizmiCoder
body:
Hi Patrik, great work!
My task: I have several pictures of a patient and I also have the 3D segmented soft tissue model of this patient. Now I want to add the texture to the 3D model so that not only the face but the entire head is textured, or at least most of the head (hair, ears). Right now eos is giving me just the textured face, logically. What would be the best approach to solve this problem? I'm new to this topic and would appreciate any advice.

title: Request for Protobuf Version Requirement in CMakeLists.txt
author: tesmachina
body:
I encountered an issue while trying to build a project using CMake and Vcpkg. The CMakeLists.txt file of the project in question uses find_package to locate Protobuf without specifying a version. This has caused issues when trying to configure the project, as it fails to find a compatible version of Protobuf.

My current Protobuf version is 3.21.12, and I would like to ensure compatibility. Could you please specify the required version of Protobuf for this project in the CMakeLists.txt file or provide guidance on the appropriate version to use? This would greatly help users who are trying to build the project using Vcpkg or other package managers.

Thank you for your assistance in resolving this matter.

title: Remove eigen3-nnls submodule
author: patrikhuber
body: The NNLS algorithm is now in Eigen https://gitlab.com/libeigen/eigen/-/merge_requests/798.

title: Re-add contour fitting to fit-model-ceres
author: patrikhuber
body: The contour fitting is commented out at the moment in fit-model-ceres. It needs extracting the yaw angle from the rotation quaternion, which Eigen currently doesn't have a function for. It could be re-added easily, we just need a reliable way to convert from the estimated rotation quaternion to a yaw angle.

title: Tune fit-model-ceres's cost function weights
author: patrikhuber
body: To find good weights, `ceres::ResidualBlockId` can be used to store the IDs to each added residual blocks. Then, one can use `ceres::Problem::EvaluateOptions` and `ceres::Problem::Evaluate(...)` to retrieve the values of each individual term. We don't do that in the example for simplicity reasons. But we should perhaps still provide adequate example weights.

title: Adding custom 3dmm
author: leg0m4n
body:
Thank you so much for your work! Amazing library, has been really helpful with my project.

I was wondering how possible is it to add custom 3dmm? For example, ICT-FaceKit(https://github.com/ICT-VGL/ICT-FaceKit), they have a very detailed model with lots of blendshapes. How would you approach such problem?

Thanks!

title: Build error with toml11 from Homebrew: no member named 'Table' 'Integer' 'String'
author: ilciavo
body:
When I try to build I get the following errors:
```
In file included from /Users/leo/Desktop/Coding/C++/eos/examples/fit-model-simple.cpp:23:
/Users/leo/Desktop/Coding/C++/eos/include/eos/core/LandmarkMapper.hpp:77:53: error: no member named 'Table' in namespace 'toml'
        const auto mappings_table = toml::get<toml::Table>(data.at("landmark_mappings"));
                                              ~~~~~~^
/Users/leo/Desktop/Coding/C++/eos/include/eos/core/LandmarkMapper.hpp:86:33: error: no member named 'Integer' in 'toml::value_t'
            case toml::value_t::Integer:
                 ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^
/Users/leo/Desktop/Coding/C++/eos/include/eos/core/LandmarkMapper.hpp:89:33: error: no member named 'String' in 'toml::value_t'
            case toml::value_t::String:

3 errors generated.
make[2]: *** [examples/CMakeFiles/fit-model-simple.dir/fit-model-simple.cpp.o] Error 1

```
```
In file included from /Users/leo/Desktop/Coding/C++/eos/include/eos/fitting/fitting.hpp:34:
/Users/leo/Desktop/Coding/C++/eos/include/eos/fitting/contour_correspondence.hpp:167:52: error: no member named 'Table' in namespace 'toml'
        const auto contour_table = toml::get<toml::Table>(data.at("contour_landmarks"));
                                             ~~~~~~^
/Users/leo/Desktop/Coding/C++/eos/include/eos/fitting/contour_correspondence.hpp:179:33: error: no member named 'Integer' in 'toml::value_t'
            case toml::value_t::Integer:
                 ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^
/Users/leo/Desktop/Coding/C++/eos/include/eos/fitting/contour_correspondence.hpp:182:33: error: no member named 'String' in 'toml::value_t'
            case toml::value_t::String:
                 ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^
/Users/leo/Desktop/Coding/C++/eos/include/eos/fitting/contour_correspondence.hpp:197:33: error: no member named 'Integer' in 'toml::value_t'
            case toml::value_t::Integer:
                 ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^
/Users/leo/Desktop/Coding/C++/eos/include/eos/fitting/contour_correspondence.hpp:200:33: error: no member named 'String' in 'toml::value_t'
            case toml::value_t::String:
                 ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^
5 errors generated.
make[2]: *** [examples/CMakeFiles/fit-model-multi.dir/fit-model-multi.cpp.o] Error 1
make[1]: *** [examples/CMakeFiles/fit-model-multi.dir/all] Error 2

```
Changing to `toml::table`, `toml::value_t::integer`, and `toml::value_t::string` seems to solve the errors. 
I'm using toml11 3.7.1 from Homebrew  
Cheers

title: Build error macOS Monterrey: template parameter redefines default argument
author: ilciavo
body:
First, thanks for sharing this cool project.
When I try to build, I get the following error:
```
Consolidate compiler generated dependencies of target fit-model-multi
[ 37%] Building CXX object examples/CMakeFiles/fit-model-multi.dir/fit-model-multi.cpp.o
In file included from /Users/leo/Desktop/Coding/C++/eos/examples/fit-model-multi.cpp:31:
In file included from /Users/leo/Desktop/Coding/C++/eos/include/eos/render/render.hpp:27:
/Users/leo/Desktop/Coding/C++/eos/include/eos/render/SoftwareRenderer.hpp:400:42: error: template parameter redefines default argument
template <typename T, glm::precision P = glm::defaultp>
                                         ^
/Users/leo/Desktop/Coding/C++/eos/include/eos/render/SoftwareRenderer.hpp:56:42: note: previous default template argument defined here
template <typename T, glm::precision P = glm::defaultp>
                                         ^
1 error generated.
make[2]: *** [examples/CMakeFiles/fit-model-multi.dir/fit-model-multi.cpp.o] Error 1
make[1]: *** [examples/CMakeFiles/fit-model-multi.dir/all] Error 2

```

The error comes from include/eos/render/SoftwareRenderer.hpp, which I've managed to compile after commenting this section:

```
// Forward declarations (these functions should probably be moved into detail/):
template <typename T, glm::precision P = glm::defaultp>
std::vector<detail::Vertex<T, P>>
clip_polygon_to_plane_in_4d(const std::vector<detail::Vertex<T, P>>& vertices,
                            const glm::tvec4<T, P>& plane_normal);
```
I'm using clang 13.1.6 and homebrew versions of the following libraries `cereal`, `eigen`, `glm`, and `toml11`

Cheers

title: Error loading the Morphable Model: Error opening given file: ./bfm2017/bfm2017-1_bfm_nomouth.bin
author: XIAO-RUI-RUI
body:
I follow the step in github. When I used the latest convert-bfm2017-to-eos.py(7ec85e6028dfcdf959c87ac7d926681121e55022), there is error follow:
`Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "convert-bfm2017-to-eos.py", line 53, in <module>
    texture_triangle_indices=[])  # uv-coordinates can be added here
TypeError: __init__(): incompatible constructor arguments. The following argument types are supported:
    1. eos.morphablemodel.MorphableModel(shape_model: eos.morphablemodel.PcaModel, color_model: eos.morphablemodel.PcaModel, texture_coordinates: List[List[float[2]]] = [])
    2. eos.morphablemodel.MorphableModel(shape_model: eos.morphablemodel.PcaModel, expression_model: Union[eos.morphablemodel.PcaModel, List[eos.morphablemodel.Blendshape]], color_model: eos.morphablemodel.PcaModel, texture_coordinates: List[List[float[2]]] = [])

Invoked with: <eos.morphablemodel.PcaModel object at 0x000001551C1B1830>, <eos.morphablemodel.PcaModel object at 0x000001551C1328B0>, <eos.morphablemodel.PcaModel object at 0x000001551C171870>; kwargs: vertex_definitions=None, texture_coordinates=[], texture_triangle_indices=[]`

---
Because the the problem, I used the **eos-py1.16.0**, the problem is disappear. But when I used the **bfm2017-1_bfm_nomouth.bin** in one program, another problem come up with:
`Error loading the Morphable Model: Error opening given file: ./bfm2017/bfm2017-1_bfm_nomouth.bin`
That all.
